  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX History (French Revolution, Socialism in Europe, Nazism)",
      "source_text": "Outline political map of France/World. Locate/label/identify Bordeaux, Nantes, Paris, Marseille; Major countries of First World War and Second World War.",
      "related_unit": "Events and Processes",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 1-3"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography (India Size, Physical Features, Drainage)",
      "source_text": "Locate/label: India States/Capitals, Tropic of Cancer, Standard Meridian; Mountain Ranges, Peaks, Plateaus, Coastal Plains; Rivers and Lakes.",
      "related_unit": "Physical and Human Geography of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 1-3"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX History (Nazism)",
      "source_text": "Outline Political Map of World. Locate/label Major countries of Second World War Axis and Allied Powers.",
      "related_unit": "Events and Processes",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 3"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography (Climate, Population)",
      "source_text": "Locate/label: Annual rainfall in India, Monsoon wind direction; Population density of all states.",
      "related_unit": "Physical and Human Geography of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 4-5"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X History (Nationalism)",
      "source_text": "Locate/label: Congress sessions (1920 Calcutta, Nagpur, Madras); Satyagraha movements (Kheda, Champaran, Ahmedabad); Jallianwala Bagh; Dandi March.",
      "related_unit": "Events and processes",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter II Nationalism in India"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Resources)",
      "source_text": "Identify Major Soil Types. Locate: Salal, Bhakra Nangal, Tehri, Rana Pratap Sagar, Sardar Sarovar, Hirakund, Nagarjun Sagar, Tungabhadra.",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 1-2"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Agriculture)",
      "source_text": "Identify: Major areas of Rice/Wheat; Largest/Major producer states of Sugarcane, Tea, Coffee, Rubber, Cotton and Jute.",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 4"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Minerals)",
      "source_text": "Identify: Iron Ore Mines, Coal Mines, Oil Fields. Locate/label Power Plants Thermal and Nuclear.",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 5"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Manufacturing)",
      "source_text": "Locating and labeling: Cotton textile Industries, Iron and Steel Plants, Software technology Parks.",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 6"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Lifelines)",
      "source_text": "Locating/Labeling: Major Sea Ports, International Airports.",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 7"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities (Class X)",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Subject Enrichment Activity (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Project work (Interdisciplinary)-Disaster Management",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Disaster Management",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives include creating awareness, preparing to face situations, and ensuring participation in disaster risk reduction plans.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Forest Society & Natural Vegetation",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with Chapter 4 of History (Forest, Society and Colonialism) and Chapter 5 of Geography (Natural Vegetation and Wildlife). Compare forest situations in pre-colonial, colonial, post-colonial eras.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods, Economies and Societies / Physical and Human Geography",
      "related_chapter": "History Ch IV & Geography Ch V"
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Globalisation and Lifelines of Economy",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with History Chapter III (Making of a Global World), Geography Chapter 7 (Lifelines of National Economy), Economics Chapter 4 (Globalisation). Analyse implications, transportation as lifeline.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods / Resources and Development",
      "related_chapter": "History Ch III, Geo Ch VII, Eco Ch IV"
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Project Work: Consumer Rights/Social Issues/Sustainable Development",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development. Objectives include gaining insight into themes and enhancing life skills.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class IX Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Class X History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class X Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class X Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class X Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment Class IX/X: 20 Marks total (Periodic Test 5, Multiple Assessment 5, Subject Enrichment/Project Work 5, Portfolio 5)",
      "Board Exam Question Paper Design: MCQs (1 Mark) - 20 marks; Long Answer (2-3 Marks) - 8+15=23 marks? No, text says 4x2=8 and 5x3=15. Case Study (4 Marks) - 12 marks. Long Answer (5 Marks) - 20 marks. Map Pointing - 5 marks.",
      "Total Board Exam: 80 Marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Chapter 5 Pastoralists in the Modern World (Class IX History): Assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur (Class IX Economics): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter IV Natural Vegetation and Wildlife Map Pointing (Class IX Geography): Only map pointing to be evaluated in the annual examination",
      "Chapter IV The Age of Industrialisation (Class X History): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Subtopics 2-4.4 Making of a Global World: Evaluated in Board Examination, Subtopic 1-1.3 also included but specific evaluation noted for subtopics"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Refer to NCF-2023 pages 320-323",
      "NEP 2020 emphasis on democratic outlook, liberty, equality, justice, diversity.",
      "Interdisciplinary projects must be done at school only (April to September). Carryover of project to home strictly avoided.",
      "Projects and models should be made from eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.",
      "Project records maintained for three months after result declaration.",
      "Use maps available on Govt. of India website.",
      "Art integration is required in projects (PowerPoint, Exhibition, Skit, etc.).",
      "Competencies to be developed: Collaboration, Analytical skills, Evaluation, Synthesis, Creative solutions."
    ]
  }
}
